_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: LOCAL City of Cocoa, FL — 5-official cluster (Mayor Blake + D1 Goins + D2 Hearn + D3 Barringer + D4 Koss). One evidence_local (Blake — 2 HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach.q0 + .q3 anchored to documented FLC Home Rule Hero 2023+2025+2026 record, current FLC President 2024-25). Three roster_verified D-confirmed council incumbents (Goins, Hearn, Koss — all listed on BrevardDems Elected Officials page; party 'D' set on that basis), 30/30 NULL each because none have public theology/culture-cell positions documented. ONE STALENESS FIX: Matthew Barringer was appointed to D3 in late 2023 to fill an unexpired term ending Nov 2024; Patricia Weeks won the Nov 2024 election and was sworn in as D3 + Deputy Mayor — Wikipedia source that seeded Barringer's record is stale, cocoafl.gov is authoritative — Barringer status set to 'former' + candidacy_status 'not_running'; Patricia Weeks needs to be added to the Cocoa queue as a NEW record (out of scope this batch — engine has no add-candidate path; flag for next sweep). Party flips set defensibly from documented public Democratic-party affiliation lists (BrevardDems), NOT inferred from votes. Blake party stays null — institutional FLC President role + FLC Home Rule Hero is municipal-bipartisan, no Brevard D or R endorsement found. HIGH BAR discipline preserved: Hearn's Brevard County firefighter/EMT employment is biography NOT TRUE on PJ.q0; Koss's police advisory board work is governance NOT TRUE on PJ.q0; Goins's BCU football / construction-industry / NAACP / Youth-Football work is biography NOT TRUE on any cell. Cocoa is NOT a sanctuary city (FL SB 168 2019 preempts statewide) and has NOT passed a 2A sanctuary resolution — both verified across all 5 agent searches; FL HB 543 (2023) occupies constitutional carry at state level. Notable structural finding: 4 of 4 currently-seated Cocoa officials (Blake, Goins, Hearn, Koss) are Democratic-aligned per BrevardDems — Cocoa is a D-majority small Brevard municipality despite nonpartisan elections; Blake is the bipartisan institutional figure (FLC President) on top of that. Confidence calls follow refine-scorecard SKILL.md.

profile.background: Michael C. 'Mike' Blake is the elected at-large Mayor of the City of Cocoa, Florida (Brevard County, pop ~17K), and a lifelong Cocoa resident. He won re-election Nov 5, 2024 defeating George Allen Cowart 62.66% (5,081) to 37.34% (3,028) for a term running through the next regular city election in 2028. His tenure on Cocoa's elected body spans 17+ years: City Council 1998-2001, Mayor 2004-2012 (two terms), District 1 Councilman 2014-June 2016, and Mayor again from 2020 to present. By profession he is a retired Cocoa High School educator (retired early 2022). Outside city hall he has chaired the Cocoa Community Redevelopment Agency and Cocoa Housing Authority, led the Space Coast League of Cities, sat on the Florida Municipal Insurance Trust Board, and rose through the Florida League of Cities (FLC) ladder: 2nd Vice President 2022-23, 1st Vice President 2023-24, and FLC President 2024-25. FLC named him a Home Rule Hero in 2023, 2025, and 2026 for advocacy during legislative sessions to push back state preemption of municipal authority. Religion / family / military / education credentials are NOT FOUND in any public source reviewed.

michael-c-blake.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: Florida-League-of-Cities institutional municipalist — current FLC President (2024-25 term) and 3-time Home Rule Hero (2023, 2025, 2026) whose entire public footprint is local-government home-rule / anti-state-preemption advocacy, with NO documented engagement on culture-war / theology / constitutional-rights rubric cells. Two HARD TRUE: (1) refuse_state_overreach.q0 — recurring FLC Home Rule Hero Award recipient explicitly recognized for advocacy 'to promote local voices making local choices' against state preemption of municipal authority during legislative sessions; (2) refuse_state_overreach.q3 — FLC presidency + Home Rule Hero pattern is verbatim subsidiarity ('local voices making local choices' = lowest-competent-level decision-making). 28 NULL — retired-teacher mayor of a small Brevard coastal city, no public statements located on life/marriage/family/election-integrity/border/2A. Routine police budget oversight (Cocoa runs its own PD, not a BCSO contract) does NOT meet HIGH BAR for public_justice.q0. Cocoa has NOT issued Pride proclamations of record (no FALSE on biblical_marriage.q4), NOT passed 2A sanctuary (no TRUE on self_defense.q1), is NOT a sanctuary city (FL SB 168 2019 preempts at state level). Party NULL — institutional FLC President role + Home Rule Hero is bipartisan municipal; no BREC or BDEC endorsement found and Ballotpedia 2024 race documents zero endorsements. Currency: 25-point 2024 re-election margin + 2026 Home Rule Hero dated to current legislative session = high-confidence still-in-office.

set.office: City Council, District 1 (Deputy Mayor), City of Cocoa

profile.background: James 'Alex' Goins is the District 1 Councilmember for the City of Cocoa, Florida and currently serves as Deputy Mayor (named by colleagues in November 2019). He is a Rockledge High School graduate and attended Bethune-Cookman University (HBCU, Daytona Beach) for Business Education, where he played football. Professionally he has worked 14 years for ABC Land Clearing and Development in construction and serves as Business Manager for the Mid Florida Football and Cheer Corporation. First elected to Cocoa Council Nov 2018; re-elected November 2022 with 51.06% over Ed Green (38.44%) and Demetrius Thomas (10.50%); Cocoa council terms are 4 years so his current term runs to November 2026. Prior to elected service he sat 2 years on the City of Cocoa Board of Adjustment. Family: wife Phylicia Goins; two children (Jaida Williams-Goins, Jacquez Goins); parents James and Betty Goins were community volunteers. Civic memberships: Tourism Development Council of Brevard, Brevard County Youth Football and Cheerleading, NAACP. Religion/church NOT FOUND in any public source. Publicly listed by the Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee on its official Elected Officials roster — party 'D' set on that basis.

alex-goins.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: BrevardDems-rostered Democratic small-city councilman whose entire public footprint is community-services (youth football, NAACP, tourism, construction industry) with ZERO documented policy advocacy on any of the 30 RESOLUTE rubric cells. Party signal documented: BrevardDems Elected Officials page lists him verbatim as Cocoa District 1, and he appears as named door-to-door canvasser in published Florida Democratic Party 'Knockathon' GOTV events with Mayor Mike Blake — confirmed Democrat (party 'D' set). However, per HIGH BAR rubric discipline ('party is not position'), all 30 cells = honest NULL. No on-record statements located on sanctity/marriage/family/Christian liberty/economic/elections/public justice/state overreach/border/self-defense. Construction-industry employment + Deputy Mayor designation is governance/biography, not affirmative rubric advocacy. No record located of him supporting or opposing a Cocoa Pride proclamation (none has been issued of record) or a 2A sanctuary resolution (Cocoa has not passed one). ROSTER_VERIFIED with party documented.

set.office: City Council, District 2 (Deputy Mayor), City of Cocoa

profile.background: Lavander Hearn is the District 2 Councilmember for the City of Cocoa, Florida (first elected November 2020; selected by colleagues as Deputy Mayor November 2021). He is a Cocoa native and Cocoa High School alumnus; attended Eastern Florida State College (Cocoa campus) and earned an Associate of Arts in Fire Science with EMT training. Professionally he is a Brevard County Firefighter/EMT and a substitute teacher for Brevard Public Schools. Father (spouse name not surfaced). Active member of Cocoa Masonic Lodge #55 (community-volunteer involvement; lodge membership is NOT a church). As a teen he had brushes with the law and publicly credits mentorship/youth-outreach programs for redirecting him — a recurring biographical theme. Cocoa council seats are 4-year staggered terms; first elected Nov 2020 → re-election cycle Nov 2024 → next term to Nov 2028 (no special election or resignation news located; presumed re-elected or returned unopposed). Publicly listed by the Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee on its official Elected Officials roster with his direct contact info — party 'D' set on that basis. Religion / Christian-church affiliation NOT FOUND in any public source.

lavander-hearn.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: BrevardDems-rostered Democratic firefighter/EMT council Deputy Mayor whose public statement footprint is sparse — limited to a rent-notice-ordinance ask (suggesting MORE state/federal action on rent volatility, which trends slightly AWAY from subsidiarity but is a single issue-management quote, not a rubric-scoreable refuse_state_overreach FALSE) and procedural budget-presentation requests. Party documented: BrevardDems Elected Officials page lists him verbatim as Cocoa District 2 with email Lavander.Hearn@yahoo.com and phone 321-604-2291 — confirmed Democrat (party 'D' set). However, per HIGH BAR rubric discipline ('biography is not advocacy; party is not position'), all 30 cells = honest NULL. Firefighter/EMT employment is first-responder biography, NOT affirmative police advocacy meeting public_justice.q0 HIGH BAR (no Back-the-Blue resolution, no Coffee-with-a-Cop sponsorship, no floor advocacy beyond routine appropriations). Substitute-teacher employment is biography, not school-choice advocacy. Masonic Lodge membership ≠ Christian church. No cells reach TRUE; no cells reach FALSE. ROSTER_VERIFIED with party documented.

set.office: City Council, District 3, City of Cocoa (appointed Oct 2023; term ended Nov 2024)

profile.background: Matthew 'Matt' Barringer was appointed by the City of Cocoa Council in late 2023 to fill the District 3 unexpired term following the resignation of the prior councilmember. The vacancy notice was posted by the City of Cocoa on October 11, 2023 with applications due October 18, 2023; the appointment was explicitly for 'the remainder of the unexpired term which expires in November 2024.' Per third-party profile aggregators his credentials include a Doctor of Education with prior M.Ed. and B.A. from UCF, prior service as a U.S. Army Sergeant, work as an Assistant Principal/Program Director in Career & Technical Education, and a community-liaison role at VITAS Healthcare (caution: a SEPARATE Matt Barringer ran for Titusville Council Seat 1 and is/was a Space Coast Jr/Sr High teacher; do NOT conflate). His campaign FB page posted July 17, 2024 'Thank you Cocoa for the privilege to serve our City!' consistent with end of appointed-term service. The Nov 5, 2024 Cocoa election was won by Patricia Weeks (a Brevard real-estate professional), who was sworn in approximately August 29, 2024 and elected to the full term in November 2024; Weeks now serves as D3 + Deputy Mayor. Religion/church / family / specific election margin NOT FOUND. Barringer has NO public party affiliation documented (NOT listed on BrevardDems Elected Officials page, where Mayor Blake / Goins / Hearn / Koss appear).

matthew-barringer.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: STALENESS-FIX — Barringer served a brief appointed District 3 term Oct 2023 to ~Aug 2024, with the seat now held by Patricia Weeks (D3 + Deputy Mayor) since the Nov 2024 election. The original scorecard record was seeded from Wikipedia, which is STALE; cocoafl.gov is authoritative and shows Weeks as current D3. Status set to 'former' with candidacy_status 'not_running'; this removes him from the active queue. All 30 cells = honest NULL because his brief tenure produced no documented rubric-level positions on the issues this scorecard tracks (FB social footprint largely login-walled; no press coverage on rubric-relevant questions). No party affiliation documented (NOT on BrevardDems alongside the 4 other Cocoa officials, which is a meaningful negative signal but is not by itself confirmation of R registration; left null). DISAMBIGUATION WARNING: there is a separate Matt Barringer who ran for Titusville Council Seat 1 and is a Space Coast Jr/Sr High teacher; do not conflate. NEXT-QUEUE FLAG: Patricia Weeks (D3 + Deputy Mayor since Nov 2024) needs a new candidate record added to scorecard.json; engine has no add-candidate path so this is flagged for a future hand-add sweep (not this batch's scope).

set.office: City Council, District 4, City of Cocoa

profile.background: Lorraine Koss has served as District 4 Councilperson for the City of Cocoa, Florida since 2018; re-elected November 2022 defeating Jennifer Kenny 62.11% to 37.89%. Profession: retired Deputy Director of the U.S. Water Alliance; prior Senior Manager at the Water Environment Federation. Education: B.A. Communications, Trinity University; M.A. Environmental Management, Duke University. Family: married to Larry Koss (Brevard native, Kennedy Space Center technical engineer working with NASA on space-food water use); two adult sons (the 2022 candidate profile notes military sons with five Iraq tours); one grandson. Volunteer / committee history: founder and first chair of Cocoa's Sustainability Advisory Board, Chair of the Utilities Advisory Board, founder of Space Coast Solar Coop (2015), Brevard County Commissioner's appointee to Save Our Indian River Lagoon Citizen Oversight Committee, Space Coast Economic Development Commission board, Space Coast Transportation Planning Organization (Executive Committee), 2024-2025 Florida League of Cities Utilities and Natural Resources Committee. Cocoa terms are 4 years so she is likely on the November 2026 ballot. Publicly listed by the Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee on its official Elected Officials roster ('District 4 Lorraine Koss 321-474-8445') and endorsed by Sierra Club in 2022 — party 'D' set on that basis. Religion / Christian-church affiliation NOT FOUND.

lorraine-koss.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: BrevardDems-rostered Democratic civic-environmental incumbent — water/stormwater/Indian River Lagoon/sustainability/smart-growth single-lane councilwoman with Duke Environmental Management credential and U.S. Water Alliance career — whose entire public footprint clusters on environmental policy with ZERO documented engagement on culture-war / theology / constitutional-rights rubric cells. Party documented: BrevardDems Elected Officials page lists her by name + phone — confirmed Democrat (party 'D' set). However, per HIGH BAR rubric discipline ('biography is not advocacy; party is not position'), all 30 cells = honest NULL. Police advisory board work (helped create the board to 'bridge gap between community and PD') is governance posture, NOT the HIGH-BAR affirmative LE backing rubric requires → public_justice.q0 NULL. FLC Legislative Policy Committee / Utilities & Natural Resources Committee service is process, NOT asserted local-control posture vs state mandates → refuse_state_overreach.q0 NULL (Blake's Home Rule Hero is the demonstrated TRUE on the council). No record located of her supporting or opposing a Cocoa Pride proclamation (none has been issued) or a 2A sanctuary (Cocoa has not passed one). ROSTER_VERIFIED with party documented.
